title: viola nemorum
intro: Viola nemorum, also known as the wood violet or dog violet, is a perennial flowering plant species in the genus Viola. Native to Europe, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding digestion. Herbal preparations of Viola nemorum, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to make teas, salves, and other remedies that can be applied topically or ingested to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
